global-Erasmus+ Staff Teaching Mobility (STA)

Erasmus+ Program enables university academic staff to spend teaching periods at the partner universities via bilateral agreements in which the staff mobility for teaching assignments is foreseen. Bilateral agreement is required for staff exchange program.

The purposes of STA may be summarized as follows:

to provide teaching staff with opportunities for personal & professional development

to share expertise in specialist areas within teacher education, to deepen mutual understanding of different European Education Systems and to raise the profile of the European Dimension in Education

to allow students who are not able to participate in mobility to benefit from the knowledge and expertise of staff in other European institutions.

Who is Eligible to Apply?

Faculty/Administrative staff members must be full-time/part-time employees of Kadir Has University (KHAS). The staff members who have a contract with KHAS can also participate in the program even though the employee does not have a tenure at KHAS.

Important Note: The staff who have a contract with the higher education institution can benefit from the program in that HEI. The staff who have a permanent contract with a HEI, yet effectively work in a different HEI under a fixed term contract shall participate in the staff mobility program in the institution they effectively work, not in the institution they have an indefinite contract with.

Duration of Mobility

All the mobilities should last min 2 days and max 4 days. The activity should consists 8 hours of teaching.

Important Note:

If the activity lasts less than 2 days or the hours are less than the minimum requirement, the activity is considered as invalid.

Destinations

To be able to participate in this mobility program, your Department/Faculty must have a valid Erasmus+ staff exchange agreement with the host institution you intend to visit. If you wish to visit an institution that is not yet our partner, we urge you to inform the host institution and initiate the procedure for signing the agreement. Please note that an institution must have an Erasmus Charter to be able to take part in this mobility.

Grants and Quotas

:

Grants are paid in two installments. 80% of daily grant and 100% of travel grant are ideally paid before departure following the submission of before-mobility documents, and the remaining 20% is paid after the staff member submits all required after-mobility documents. Payments are made in Euros (€).

Daily Grant Amount

Erasmus+ Staff mobility program provides daily stipend to staff members based on the table below:

Host Country                                            Grant Amount Per Day (Euro)

Group Program Countries: Germany, Austria, Belgium, France, Denmark, Italy, Ireland, Sweden, The Netherlands, Finland, Iceland, Lichtenstein, Luxembourg, Norway, and Region 13-14 (Andorra, Monaco, Switzerland and the UK) 171€.

Group Program Countries: Cyprus, Greece, Spain, Malta, Portugal, Estonia, Slovenia, Slovakia, Latvia, Czechia 153€.

Group Program Countries Bulgaria, North Macedonia, Poland, Romania, Croatia, Lithuania, Hungary, Serbia,133€.

Travel Grant: Travel grant will be calculated via distance calculator (https://ec.europa.eu/programmes/erasmus-plus/tools/distance_en.htm ) for a round trip!

Participants can participate in the program without receiving grant. However, they have to go through the same application & selection procedures and also submit the required documents.

Additional grants are available for participants with disabilities, where participation would not be possible without extra financial support. The allowance is offered when disabilities or health conditions lead to additional mobility costs, which exceed the maximum grant allocations allowed, and which cannot be recovered from other sources. The grant covers approved actual costs. Participant’s application must include a statement from a doctor or other authority confirming the disability or health condition, its severity, the impact on the Erasmus+ mobility, additional requirements, and additional costs to be met. A detailed estimate of costs is required. The application should provide information on any other sources of financial support that the participant receives and why this is insufficient to cover extra costs.

If you have any questions about Erasmus+ Disability Grant, please send an e-mail to

[email protected]

.

Grant Deduction

Failure to complete the required after mobility documents, 30% of the grant based on total duration of mobility shall be deducted.

Failure to submit the approved certificate of participation, which is the proof for realisition of the mobility, the grant shall be refunded by the participant.

Failure to complete the online final report after mobility, 5% of the grant based on total duration of mobility shall be deducted.

If the participant does not complete the min. duration of mobility (2 days) and return Turkey without any force majeure reason, the mobility activity will be considered ineligible, and the participant will be asked to return the initial grant (70%).

Criteria

* Job experience at Kadir Has University:

0-1 year: 0 point

1-3 years: +1 point

3-5 years: +3 points

5-7 years: +5 points

7-10 years: +7 points

10 years: +10 points

* Erasmus Department Coordinators at the time of application: +10 points

* Staff with disabilities are prioritized (The statement from a doctor or other relevant authority confirming the special needs should be submitted during the application period) (+10 points)

* Spouse and/or Children of martyr people and/or veteran staff members are prioritized (if proving documents are provided in the application) (+10 points)

* Participants who benefitted from the mobility before (-10 points)

* Holding a residence for outgoing country (-5 points)

* Signing new Erasmus+ or exchange agreement (including student mobility): + 5 points each (max. 15 points)

* Those who choose to visit countries and institutions that previously have not been visited, or rarely been visited (Estonia, Iceland, Malta, Denmark, Sweden, Finland and Luxembourg) subject to change in application term (+5 points)

Documents required

BEFORE

mobility:

Official invitation letter (signed and stamped by the receiving institution)

Erasmus+ HE Staff Mobility agreement – teaching (confirmed by parties)

Erasmus+ HE Staff Mobility Grant Agreement

Euro account at Garanti Bankası

Documents required

AFTER

mobility:

Certificate of Attendance (original document confirmed by the receiving institution)

EU Online Survey (sent via email)

*Activities related to Artificial Intelligence within the scope of the National AI Strategy: (+5 points)

https://cbddo.gov.tr/SharedFolderServer/Genel/File/TR-UlusalYZStratejisi2021-2025.pdf
